title: bryodesma underwoodii
side_effects: Bryodesma underwoodii can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory issues. Ingesting parts of the plant can cause gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can lead to liver and kidney damage, and in severe cases, even organ failure. If ingested or applied topically in excessive amounts, bryodesma underwoodii can also cause headaches, dizziness, and fatigue.
